clinical_history: 64-year-old man with chest pain at rest.  The ECG shows all of the following except:
diagnosis: b) Sinus rhythm with baseline artifact is present, simulating atrial fibrillation.  Such artifact may be caused by a variety of factors including poor electrode contact, muscle tremor and electrical interference.  A single  premature ventricular complex (PVC) is present with a compensatory pause such that the RR interval surrounding the PVC is twice as long as the preceding sinus RR interval.  Evidence of a prior anterior MI is present with pathologic Q waves  in V1-V3.  Borderline low precordial voltage is a nonspecific finding.  Cardiac catheterization showed a 90~ stenosis in the proximal portion the left anterior descending coronary, which was treated with angioplasty and stenting.   The broad P wave in lead V1, with a prominent negative component (terminal negative force), is consistent with left atrial abnormality.
